zh.lifeSummary: Matilda 14GGM Maude Maddie Mynne Jansz Janes Mynne於1503年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可蕾托 福藍善姆。她於大約1518年與Edward (MYall-Minn-Mynns) MYNNE在英國英格蘭諾福爾可蕾托 福藍善姆結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1573年在她的家鄉享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: Mynne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German:
variant of   Menn  .
habitational name from Menne in Westphalia.

variants.name: Menning
meanings: German:
patronymic from   Menne  .
habitational name from a place called Menning or Menningen in southern Germany.

variants.name: Menn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Anatoly, Boris, Dmitry, Svetlana.  German Kurt, Otto.
German: variant of   Mein  .
Breton (Finistère; mainly Le Menn): nickname from menn ‘kid, young goat’, perhaps denoting an agile person.

variants.name: Menner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Lorant.
German and Hungarian: occupational name from Middle High German mener ‘cattle drover’.
German: habitational name for someone from Menne (see   Menne  ).

nameMeanings.name: Jansz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Dutch: relationship name, patronymic from the personal name Jan.

nameMeanings.name: Janes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): variant of Jane , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s (see Jayne ).

Slovenian and Croatian (of Slovenian origin) (Janeš): variant of Slovenian Janež, from an old equivalent of the Latin personal name Johannes (see John ), or a topographic name or metonymic occupational name from janež ‘anise’. Compare Yanes .
Czech (Janeš, Jáneš): from a pet form of the personal name Jan .

variants.name: Janis
meanings: English: variant of   Janes  .
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Jean   ‘John’.

Czech and Slovak (mainly Janiš); Polish (also Janiś): from a derivative of the personal name Jan ‘John’ (Slovak Ján). In North America, this surname may also be an altered form of the Polish variant Janisz. The homonymous Sorbian cognate Janiš is found in Germanized and other Americanized forms, only (see Janisch and Janish ).

variants.name: Janice
meanings: English: variant of   Janes  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jaime, Orlando, Alicia, Isidro, Julio, Miguel, Rafael.
English (Devon and Cornwall): variant of Jayne . As a personal name, Jane was not specialized as a female form until the 17th century.
Catalan (Jané): variant of Gener ‘January’.

nameMeanings.name: Mynne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German:
variant of   Menn  .
habitational name from Menne in Westphalia.

variants.name: Menning
meanings: German:
patronymic from   Menne  .
habitational name from a place called Menning or Menningen in southern Germany.

variants.name: Menn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Anatoly, Boris, Dmitry, Svetlana.  German Kurt, Otto.
German: variant of   Mein  .
Breton (Finistère; mainly Le Menn): nickname from menn ‘kid, young goat’, perhaps denoting an agile person.

variants.name: Menner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Lorant.
German and Hungarian: occupational name from Middle High German mener ‘cattle drover’.
German: habitational name for someone from Menne (see   Menne  ).

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: Maude
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Maud  , the usual spelling of the name in North America.

nameMeanings.name: Maddie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Madeleine  , also used as an independent given name.

variants.name: Madeleine
meanings: The French form of the byname of a character in the New Testament, Mary Magdalene ‘Mary of Magdala’. Magdala was a village on Lake Galilee, a few miles north of Tiberias. The woman ‘which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities’ (Luke 8:2) was given this name in the Bible to distinguish her from other bearers of the very common name Mary . It was widely accepted in Christian folk belief that she was the same person as the repentant sinner who washed Christ's feet with her tears in the previous chapter (Luke 7), but there is no support in the text for this identification.

en.lifeSummary: Matilda 14GGM Maude Maddie Mynne Jansz Janes Mynne was born in 1503, in Little Fransham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. She married Edward (MYall-Minn-Mynns) MYNNE about 1518, in Little Fransham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died in 1573, in her hometown, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Matilda 14GGM Maude Maddie Mynne Jansz Janes Mynne est née en 1503, à Little Fransham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Edward (MYall-Minn-Mynns) MYNNE environ 1518, à Little Fransham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1573, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda 14GGM Maude Maddie Mynne Jansz Janes Mynneは1503年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクリットル フランシャムで生まれた。約1518年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクリットル フランシャムでEdward (MYall-Minn-Mynns) MYNNEと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1573年に故郷で70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda 14GGM Maude Maddie Mynne Jansz Janes Mynne(은)는 1503년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 리틀 프랜샤에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1518년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 리틀 프랜샤에서 Edward (MYall-Minn-Mynns) MYNNE(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1573년 그녀의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Matilda 14GGM Maude Maddie Mynne Jansz Janes Mynne родилась в 1503 г. в местности литл франшам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edward (MYall-Minn-Mynns) MYNNE около 1518 г. в местности литл франшам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1573 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 70 лет.
